title: Writing for non-technical readers
question: Who are decisions written for, and how should titles and details read for them?
rationale: Write decisions for a non-technical reader: titles name the everyday topic in plain words, with the precise tool kept in the option labels and rationale (jargon explained on first use). Chosen over technical titles, which hide the topic, and dropping precision, which loses the answer.

tradeoffs.note: the list explains itself without knowing the tools involved
the exact tool is still recorded, just inside the option and rationale
the writer must translate jargon on first use
the exact tool is visible right in the title
a tool name in the title hides the topic from non-experts

options.label: Plain everywhere, drop the tool names

tradeoffs.note: omitting the tool loses the actual decision
